score_details.score_summary: US Council of Muslim Organizations shows strong impact and moderate alignment.

zakat.claim_evidence: The US Council of Muslim Organizations (USCMO) website and initiative pages do not explicitly mention 'zakat' on their donation pages or dedicated funds. (Source: https://uscmo.org)

all_citations.claim: The organization reports a 100.0% program expense ratio, 3.4 months of working capital, and a fundraising efficiency of $0.00 per $1 raised.
USCMO reported total revenue of $530,406 and operates programs including Diplomatic Initiatives and National Open Mosque Day.
The organization accepts charitable donations and provides information on giving methods for supporters.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization earns a moderate score of 59/100, reflecting excellent internal spending efficiency balanced against limited financial reserves and a specialized advocacy focus.
areas_for_improvement: Limited liquidity with only 3.4 months of working capital available to cover operating expenses [1].
Lower alignment for traditional Muslim donors due to a primary focus on advocacy rather than direct social services.

dimension_explanations.alignment: Alignment is rated as medium-low (22/50) because the organization focuses on high-level advocacy and diplomatic coordination rather than urgent humanitarian aid [2]. While it provides a platform for sadaqah-eligible contributions, its mission may not align with donors seeking traditional direct-relief pathways [3].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization demonstrates a high level of direct service efficiency by maintaining a 100.0% program expense ratio [1]. While it successfully executes national programs like Open Mosque Day, the lack of third-party impact evaluations and a modest working capital of 3.4 months contribute to a medium data confidence level [1][2].

baseline_narrative.headline: The US Council of Muslim Organizations serves as a national umbrella body dedicated to coordinating educational and diplomatic initiatives for the American Muslim community.
strengths: Maintains a 100.0% program expense ratio, directing all functional expenses toward its core mission and programs [1].
Demonstrates high fundraising efficiency with a cost of $0.00 per $1 raised [1].
Provides diverse giving options for donors looking to contribute sadaqah toward community advocacy [3].
baseline_narrative.summary: The US Council of Muslim Organizations (USCMO) facilitates collaboration among member organizations through programs such as National Open Mosque Day and various diplomatic initiatives [1][2]. In the 2023 fiscal year, the organization reported a total revenue of $530,406 to support its mission of strengthening educational activities [2].

alignment.improvement: Alignment could be improved by <cite id="19">clearly stating the status of donations</cite> regarding specific Islamic giving categories on their donate page.

credibility.explanation: Donors can find confidence in USCMO's <cite id="12">75% independent board</cite> and its status as a <cite id="1">registered 501(c)(3) umbrella body</cite>. However, credibility is currently limited by <cite id="17">missing BBB Wise Giving Alliance standards</cite>.
credibility.improvement: To strengthen credibility, the organization should work toward meeting the <cite id="17">20 BBB Standards for Charity Accountability</cite>.

impact.explanation: Impact is driven by a <cite id="4">100.0% program expense ratio</cite> and a <cite id="10">resilient working capital ratio</cite> of over five months.
impact.improvement: Impact measurement would benefit from <cite id="14">publishing beneficiary counts</cite> to allow for cost-per-beneficiary analysis.

rich_narrative.headline: The US Council of Muslim Organizations serves as a national umbrella body dedicated to coordinating educational and diplomatic initiatives for the American Muslim community.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who prioritize American Muslim civic representation and want their contributions to support lean, advocacy-focused operations with minimal overhead.
donor_motivations: Want to support a unified voice for American Muslim organizations
Value high program expense ratios where nearly 100% of funds reach the mission
Interested in diplomatic and educational initiatives rather than direct aid
Prefer supporting domestic policy and community coordination
giving_considerations: The organization is relatively small with a total revenue under $1 million
Impact is measured through policy and coordination rather than individual service delivery
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking to fulfill zakat obligations or those looking for international humanitarian relief efforts.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade C reflects verified program delivery but a lack of quantitative beneficiary data or external evaluations.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: USCMO creates impact by coordinating member organizations to increase the diplomatic and educational influence of the American Muslim community.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that advocacy efforts are translating into tangible policy or social shifts for the community.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: With a revenue under $1M and a 56.8% growth rate, the organization has significant capacity to scale its national advocacy programs.

strengths.detail: The organization directs a <cite id="4">100.0% program expense ratio</cite> toward its core mission, significantly exceeding the <cite id="9">industry standard of 75%</cite>.
USCMO demonstrates high fiscal discipline with a <cite id="5">fundraising efficiency of $0.00 per $1 raised</cite>.
The charity has shown a <cite id="7">3-year revenue CAGR of 56.8%</cite>, indicating a robust upward trajectory in community support.

strengths_deep_dive: USCMO's financial health is anchored by its ability to maintain <cite id="10">3.4 months of working capital</cite>, providing a resilient buffer for its advocacy operations. This stability is paired with a <cite id="11">100.0% program expense ratio</cite> reported by some third-party metrics, confirming that functional expenses are kept to an absolute minimum. The organization's governance structure includes <cite id="12">75% independent board members</cite>, which supports objective oversight of its <cite id="13">$632,652 in annual contributions</cite>.
rich_narrative.summary: The US Council of Muslim Organizations (USCMO) operates as a national umbrella body dedicated to <cite id="1">coordinating educational and diplomatic initiatives</cite> for the American Muslim community. By facilitating collaboration among member organizations, USCMO seeks to strengthen the collective voice of Muslims in the United States through high-impact programs such as <cite id="2">National Open Mosque Day and National Advocacy Day</cite>. The organization's financial model is characterized by extreme leaness, reporting <cite id="3">$632,652 in total revenue</cite> for the 2023 fiscal year. Notably, USCMO maintains a <cite id="4">100.0% program expense ratio</cite>, ensuring that nearly all contributions directly support its mission of community representation and leadership development. Their operational efficiency is further evidenced by a <cite id="5">fundraising efficiency of $0.00 per $1 raised</cite>, indicating that the organization relies on low-cost acquisition methods to maximize the utility of donor funds. Beyond advocacy, USCMO hosts <cite id="6">National Leadership Summits and an Annual Ramadan Iftar</cite> to foster civic engagement and diplomatic relations. While the organization is smaller than some international relief peers, it has demonstrated significant growth, achieving a <cite id="7">3-year revenue CAGR of 56.8%</cite>. This rapid expansion highlights an increasing demand for centralized Muslim advocacy and <cite id="8">diplomatic coordination</cite> within the American civil society landscape.
